title: The discovery feed is a producer/consumer split: the api-gateway writes + emails, the curator agent cards + proposes — stamp-last and propose-only make it safe
summary: WS2.3 (mig 432) built only the PRODUCER of the free-LLM discovery feed: a daily monitor that scrapes a public free-LLM index, writes newly-listed providers/models into gate_discovered_providers, and emails super_admins. It deliberately does NOT file board cards, because the production api-gateway holds no AgentBoard write credential (SpiderGate LEARNINGS) — so a consumer that DOES hold board-write is required. WS3b built that consumer: the curator agent ('Kevin', who already holds a board-write PAT) polls the feed and turns each flagged row into a research + onboarding card. The seam is two columns on the producer table: notified_at (the monitor stamps it when it emails) and kevin_carded_at (the consumer stamps it when carded). The consumer's poll predicate is exactly the migration-432 partial index idx_gdp_kevin_pending: notified_at IS NOT NULL AND kevin_carded_at IS NULL — which excludes the monitor's silent 80-row cold-seed (notified_at NULL) so the initial seed never spams the board. Two invariants keep it correct: (1) STAMP-LAST — the consumer files the card, THEN markCarded; stamping first then crashing would silently drop a provider forever, whereas stamp-last makes a crash re-card (at-least-once), and markCarded is idempotent (stamped=false HTTP 200 on a re-stamp) so at-least-once is safe. (2) PROPOSE-ONLY for a brand-new provider — onboarding hands off to author-catalog.addModel, which for an un-approved provider records a 'proposed' gate_provider_onboarding row (HTTP 202) that a human approves (POST /providers/onboarding/{name}/approve, require_super_admin_or_admin_key — NO PAT path) before the provider is wired; an already-approved provider's new model is added directly (201). The consumer surface (this skill) only lists + stamps — it never itself adds a model, keeping the discovery loop and the authoring surface cleanly separated. Lesson: when a privileged-but-credential-less service discovers work, hand it to a scoped agent via a two-timestamp seam on the row, poll the un-carded+notified slice, stamp last, and keep the risky action (new-provider onboarding) propose-only.
references: app/api/v1/admin_gate/catalog_meta.py — GET /catalog/discovery-feed (list un-carded: notified_at IS NOT NULL AND kevin_carded_at IS NULL) + POST /catalog/discovery-feed/{id}/carded (stamp kevin_carded_at, idempotent) — the consume seam this skill wraps

app/database/migrations/432_gate_discovered_providers.sql — the producer table + idx_gdp_kevin_pending partial index (the poll predicate); app/services/gate/model_discovery_monitor.py + app/services/gate/gate_monitor_service.py — the WS2.3 producer that writes rows + emails super_admins (email-only, §13d)

app/api/v1/admin_gate/catalog_meta.py — POST /admin/gate/models (add_model_card, WS2.4): approved provider -> 201 add; brand-new provider -> 202 'proposed' onboarding row; POST /providers/onboarding/{name}/approve is require_super_admin_or_admin_key (no PAT) — the human gate this consumer proposes into

author-catalog skill (@spideriq/admin-skills) — addModel, the onboarding proposal this consumer hands off to; SpiderGate LEARNINGS 'the api-gateway holds no AgentBoard write credential' (why a consumer agent is the seam)
gate-reliability-observability plan WS3b + WS2.3 (#2551), board ee38dd3d; Kevin curation board e4c11722; memory spidergate-catalog-curator-agent-initiative
